basic_details.job_title: CIET NCERT Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 21 SRF, JPF and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Central Institute of Educational Technology (CIET), NCERT, New Delhi
basic_details.post_name: Senior Research Fellow/Senior Research Associate, Junior Project Fellow, 3D Graphic Developer/Animator/Graphic Designer
basic_details.short_information: CIET-NCERT is recruiting 21 contractual posts including Senior Research Fellow, Junior Project Fellow and 3D Graphic Developer to work on various educational technology and media projects till 31st March 2027.

vacancy_details.Project Title (PAC Code): A Study of the Awareness of AI Literacy... (20.01)
Assess the Impact of Multiple Digital Education Modes... (20.02)
Development and Dissemination of Educational Media Programs (20.04)
Development of a Multilingual Language Learning Platform (20.05)
Integrating Indian Knowledge System... Graphic Novels (20.06)
Development of syllabi and textbook in Computer Science (20.08)

salary_details.senior research fellow / senior research: NET/Ph.D.: Rs.42,000/- p.m. Non-NET: Rs.40,000/- p.m.
salary_details.junior project fellow: NET/Ph.D.: Rs.37,000/- p.m. Non-NET: Rs.35,000/- p.m.

selection_process: Selection will be done through Interview. Shortlisted candidates will be informed via registered email. Candidates must report by 9:00 a.m. on the interview date (no entry after 10:00 a.m.) with all original documents.
how_to_apply: Candidates must fill the online application form using the QR code provided for each post. Separate applications are required for different posts.
Experience Certificates / Salary slips / ITR / Bank statements
Portfolio of work (Scripts, Storyboards, Animations, Graphics, etc.)
Scan the QR code given against each post and fill the online application form.
Shortlisted candidates will be informed through registered email.
Report for interview on the scheduled date by 9:00 a.m. at CIET-NCERT, New Delhi with all original documents.
important_instructions: Posts are purely temporary and contractual up to 31st March 2027. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the interview. Selected candidates may be required to join immediately. Experience in school education / e-content development is highly preferred. Time spent on M.Phil./Ph.D. will not count as experience.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form for CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026 is 28 May 2026 before 12:00 p.m. Candidates must complete the application process through the QR code or online link provided in the notification. Applications submitted after the deadline may not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 21 contractual vacancies have been announced by the Central Institute of Educational Technology (CIET), NCERT. These include posts such as Senior Research Fellow/Senior Research Associate (08 posts), Junior Project Fellow (12 posts), and 3D Graphic Developer/Animator/Graphic Designer (01 post). The recruitment is being conducted for various PAC-approved educational projects.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Educational qualifications vary according to the post applied for. Candidates applying for SRF/SRA posts must have a postgraduate degree with at least 55% marks and relevant experience, while Junior Project Fellow applicants require a postgraduate degree in a relevant field. For the 3D Graphic Developer role, candidates must possess qualifications in Fine Arts, Graphic Design, or Animation along with relevant experience.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit depends on the post category under this recruitment drive. The maximum age for Senior Research Fellow/Senior Research Associate and 3D Graphic Developer posts is 45 years, while Junior Project Fellow applicants should not exceed 40 years of age. Age relaxation will be provided to reserved categories as per Government of India norms.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered in CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a consolidated monthly remuneration based on the post and qualifications. Senior Research Fellows may receive up to Rs.42,000 per month, Junior Project Fellows can earn up to Rs.37,000 per month, and the 3D Graphic Developer position offers Rs.50,000 per month. The salary differs for NET/Ph.D. qualified and non-NET candidates.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process will be conducted through an interview only, and shortlisted candidates will be informed via their registered email ID. Applicants must report to the interview venue by 9:00 a.m., and no entry will be permitted after 10:00 a.m. Final selection will depend on qualifications, experience, and interview performance.

faq.question: Q7. How can candidates apply for CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply through the online application form available via QR code or official application link mentioned in the notification. Separate applications are required for different posts if a candidate wishes to apply for more than one position. Applicants should carefully fill all details and submit the form before the deadline.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are required for CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants must carry original and photocopies of their educational certificates, experience proof, bio-data, category certificate (if applicable), and portfolio of work for creative posts. Candidates who are currently employed should also provide a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer. Proper document verification is mandatory during the interview process.

faq.question: Q9. Are the posts in CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026 permanent?
faq.answer: No, the positions announced under CIET-NCERT Recruitment 2026 are purely temporary and contractual in nature. The engagement is expected to continue only up to 31 March 2027, depending on project requirements and performance. Candidates should note that this recruitment does not guarantee permanent employment with NCERT.
